alignments.pub_claim_text: Human behavior is consistent with contingency representations and not with traditional sensory models of working memory.
alignments.rationale: The behavioral result is preserved exactly between the preprint and the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Contingency representations can explain neurophysiological observations from the prefrontal cortex during working memory tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Task-optimized recurrent neural networks can explain neurophysiological observations from the prefrontal cortex during working memory tasks.
alignments.rationale: The publication attributes explanatory power to task-optimized RNNs rather than directly to contingency representations, changing the framing of the mechanistic explanation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study generated falsifiable predictions for neural data to identify contingency representations in neural data and to dissociate different models of working memory.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Falsifiable predictions for neural data were generated to identify contingency representations and to dissociate different models of working memory.
alignments.rationale: The contribution of producing falsifiable predictions is preserved with the same content and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings characterize a neural representational strategy that can unify working memory, planning, and context-dependent decision making.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Contingency representations can unify working memory and planning computations.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core unifying claim but narrows it to working memory and planning, omitting explicit mention of context-dependent decision making.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Connectomes with synaptic resolution are a major determinant of brain function.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Connectome-based brain modeling can reveal previously unknown anatomical structure in a neural circuit and provide insights linking network form to function.
alignments.rationale: The broad preprint claim about synaptic-resolution connectomes is rephrased in the publication to emphasize specific insights and linking form to function, narrowing and clarifying the original assertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Modules of strongly connected neurons in the larval zebrafish brainstem are specialized for different behavioral functions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Modules of strongly connected neurons are specialized for different behavioral functions, specifically the control of eye and body movements.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the module specialization claim but specifies the behaviors (eye and body movements), narrowing the scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neural network model built from the synaptic weight matrix predicts neural coding of eye position that statistically matches observations from calcium imaging.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A neural network model based on the reconstructed wiring diagram predicts cellular-resolution coding of eye position and neural dynamics.
alignments.rationale: The modeling and prediction claim is retained with equivalent meaning and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Connectome-based brain modeling can yield experimentally testable predictions of neural activity and behavior.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The synaptic wiring diagram of the larval zebrafish brainstem can predict key functional properties.
alignments.rationale: Both claims assert that the wiring diagram/model can predict functional properties and generate testable predictions; the publication restates this idea focused on the larval zebrafish brainstem.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The eye movement module is organized into two three-block cycles that support positive feedback underlying low-dimensional attractor dynamics in oculomotor control.
alignments.rationale: This specific anatomical/functional motif (two three-block cycles) appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new class of topic modelling strategies has been introduced to overcome some of the limitations of classical methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new class of topic modeling strategies has been introduced to overcome some of the limitations of classical methods.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is restated identically between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The established cancer subtype organization is well reconstructed in the inferred latent topic structure.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The established cancer subtype organization is well reconstructed in the inferred latent topic structure.
alignments.rationale: The result about subtype reconstruction is preserved unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Specific topics enriched in genes known to play a role in the corresponding disease are strongly related to the survival probability of patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Specific topics enriched in genes known to play a role in the corresponding disease are strongly related to the survival probability of patients.
alignments.rationale: The association between topics and survival is carried over without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A simple neural network classifier operating in the low dimensional topic space is able to predict with high accuracy the cancer subtype of a test expression sample.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A simple neural network classifier operating in the low dimensional topic space is able to predict with high accuracy the cancer subtype of a test expression sample.
alignments.rationale: The classifier performance claim is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Color from filling-in is best classified and best correlated with appearance by two dorsal areas, V3A and V3B/KO.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Color from filling-in is best classified and best correlated with appearance by two dorsal areas, V3A and V3B/KO.
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication texts are effectively identical in content and implication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A field of uniform chromaticity is best classified by ventral areas hV4 and LO.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A field of uniform chromaticity is best classified by ventral areas hV4 and LO.
alignments.rationale: The claim is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dynamic causal modeling revealed feedback modulation from area V3A to areas V1 and LO for filling-in.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dynamic causal modeling revealed feedback modulation from area V3A to areas V1 and LO for filling-in.
alignments.rationale: The result is carried over verbatim to the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Local and contextual influences on color appearance engage distinct neural networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Local and contextual influences on color appearance engage distinct neural networks.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion appears unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Feedback from LO modulates areas V1 and V3A for a matched uniform chromaticity.
alignments.rationale: This specific claim about LO feedback for matched uniform chromaticity does not appear in the preprint and is new in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A sensor based controller operating on artificial neurons can lead to adaptive behavior in a simulated hexapod robot.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A sensor based controller operating on artificial neurons leads to adaptive behavior in a simulated insectoid robot facing uncertain environments.
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the wording (leads vs can lead) and specifies the operating conditions (uncertain environments, insectoid), clarifying and slightly amplifying the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The decentralized solution demonstrates effective control of walking in uncertain environments for a range of behaviors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The decentralized solution produces stable footfall patterns such as 'tripod', 'tetrapod', and 'pentapod', which are stable against disturbances.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides specific stable footfall patterns and stability details, narrowing the broad 'range of behaviors' to concrete, demonstrated patterns.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This approach can account for neurophysiological results without relying on explicit CPG-like structures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach can account for neurophysiological results typically interpreted to support the idea that CPGs are essential for walking.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the same point in terms of accounting for results interpreted as evidence for essential CPGs; content is consistent but phrased differently.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The controller can be complemented with CPG-like structures for very fast walking.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not present the claim about complementing the controller with CPG-like structures for very fast walking; it is omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed neuronal architecture allows for starting or interrupting a walk more easily than CPG controlled solutions.
alignments.rationale: This claim about ease of starting/interruption relative to CPGs appears in the publication but was not in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Common properties observed in both insects and crustaceans suggest that the significance of the controller extends beyond insects.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes a broader taxonomic generalization not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our multi-task learning framework for BioNER improves performance by collectively using the training data of different types of entities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed multi-task learning framework for BioNER improves performance by collectively using training data from different types of entities.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The multi-task model achieves substantially better performance compared to state-of-the-art BioNER systems and baseline neural sequence labeling models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments on 15 benchmark BioNER datasets demonstrate that the multi-task model achieves substantially better performance compared to state-of-the-art BioNER systems and baseline neural sequence labeling models.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The large performance gains are attributed to sharing character- and word-level information among relevant biomedical entities across differently labeled corpora.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The large performance gains of the multi-task model come from sharing character- and word-level information among relevant biomedical entities across differently labeled corpora.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dendrites of FS basket cells in both hippocampus and prefrontal cortex come in two flavors: supralinear and sublinear.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dendrites of FS basket cells in both hippocampus and prefrontal cortex come in two flavors: supralinear and sublinear.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A 2-stage Artificial Neural Network (ANN), with sub- and supralinear hidden nodes, captures most of the variance in somatic firing.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A 2-stage artificial neural network (ANN), with sub- and supralinear hidden nodes, captures most of the variance in somatic firing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Altering network topology, together with density, will affect its synchronization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Altering network topology can alter the balance between intrinsically vs. synaptically driven network activity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Altering network topology can alter the balance between intrinsically vs. synaptically driven network activity.
alignments.rationale: The claim is present in both versions with the same content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neuromodulation may allow a network to shift between a more synchronized transmission mode and a more asynchronous intrinsic read-out mode.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neuromodulation may allow a network to shift between a more synchronized transmission mode and a more asynchronous intrinsic read-out mode.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural modulations of the kind and magnitude observed experimentally lead to performance changes of the kind and magnitude observed experimentally. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural modulations of the kind and magnitude observed experimentally lead to performance changes of the kind and magnitude observed experimentally. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: At earlier layers, attention applied according to tuning does not successfully propagate through the network. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Attention applied according to tuning has a weaker impact on performance than attention applied according to values computed for optimally modulating higher areas. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Biological attention might be applied at least in part to optimize function rather than strictly according to tuning. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: These presynaptic interactions preferentially boost responses to coherently expanding visual stimuli generated by colliding objects. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Multiple layers of inhibitory and excitatory presynaptic connections are needed to finely tune neural circuits for collision detection. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: A simple tensor component analysis (TCA) can extract three interconnected, low-dimensional descriptions of neural data. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Neuron factors extracted by TCA reflect cell assemblies. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: TCA reveals insights into diverse datasets derived from artificial neural networks and large-scale neural recordings. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: In tasks that require actively manipulating information, persistent activity naturally emerges from learning. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The NiDelta predictor can predict the tertiary structures of a number of target proteins with remarkable accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach presents high efficiency in protein structure determination if sparse experimental data is available. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The activation of cerebellar lobules Crus I and VI disappears during extinction when US omission becomes expected. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ANN likely reconstructs vision by seizing hidden spike patterns that represent stimuli distinctly in a two-dimensional plane.

alignments.rationale: The preprint hypothesized a low-dimensional (two-dimensional) latent structure; the publication reports reconstruction of complex, dynamic scenes and emphasizes high-dimensional population representations, changing the dimensionality claim and broadening the framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Optimal performance in transforming visual stimuli into neuronal activity is achieved in regions closely associated with vision processing.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ANN-based model is able to learn task-relevant representations embedded at a high-dimensional population level and achieve reliable decoding performance.
alignments.rationale: The publication characterizes performance in terms of learning task-relevant, high-dimensional population representations and reliable decoding, which clarifies the preprint's statement about optimal performance in vision-related regions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The C. elegans connectome exhibits symmetry in the number of synaptic partners between neuron class members.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Symmetry in the number of synaptic partners exists between neuron class members, while pronounced asymmetry is observed in the identity of these synapses.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly includes the symmetry in number of partners; this is the same empirical observation, though presented alongside additional asymmetry findings.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is pronounced asymmetry in the identity of synapses within the C. elegans connectome.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Symmetry in the number of synaptic partners exists between neuron class members, while pronounced asymmetry is observed in the identity of these synapses.
alignments.rationale: The publication pairs the identity asymmetry result with the symmetry-in-number result but preserves the original empirical claim about identity asymmetry.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Redundancy and Reachability in the C. elegans connectome are stochastically tunable by the level of network asymmetry.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Redundancy and Reachability in the connectome are stochastically tunable by the level of network asymmetry.
alignments.rationale: This modeling/result statement appears verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The C. elegans connectome favors Redundancy over Reachability due to its network asymmetry.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The connectome of Caenorhabditis elegans favors Redundancy over Reachability due to the influence of network asymmetry.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion about the connectome's preference for redundancy driven by asymmetry.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These results elucidate fundamental relations between lateralized neural connectivity and function.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The brain exhibits both bilateral symmetry and considerable asymmetry.
alignments.rationale: The publication offers a broader, higher-level framing (both symmetry and asymmetry in the brain) compared with the preprint's specific statement about lateralized connectivity and function, thus clarifying and broadening the conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The asynchronous regime is observable at low noise and becomes unstable via a sub-critical Hopf bifurcation at sufficiently large noise. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The asynchronous regime is observable at low noise and becomes unstable via a sub-critical Hopf bifurcation at sufficiently large noise. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is textually and substantively the same as the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A coexistence region between the asynchronous and the clustered regime is identified. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A coexistence region between the asynchronous and the clustered regime is identified. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this result verbatim, with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The clustered phase is characterized by population bursts in the γ-range (30-120 Hz), with neurons split into two equally populated clusters firing in alternation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The clustered phase is characterised by population bursts in the γ-range (30–120 Hz), with neurons split into two equally populated clusters firing in alternation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim matches the preprint claim in content and detail.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Single neurons display switching between the two clusters due to heterogeneity and/or noise, despite the global activity being essentially periodic. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This conclusion appears unchanged in the publication, with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A four-neuron circuit can rapidly and controllably switch between 24 unique logical functions while maintaining the same set of synaptic weights. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Personalized functional network topography predicted overall psychopathology with a correlation of r = 0.16 and permutation testing p < .001. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication quantifies the association reported in the preprint, clarifying effect size and significance while preserving the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reduced cortical representations of association networks were among the most important features of the model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Reduced representation of association networks was among the most important features for the prediction of all 4 dimensions of psychopathology. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the preprint claim by specifying the prediction applied across all four dimensions, but the substantive importance of reduced association-network representation is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The variation in functional topography is related to major dimensions of psychopathology in youth. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Individual differences in functional network topography in association networks is related to overall psychopathology in youth. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both claims link individual variation in topography to psychopathology in youth with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Considering systematic differences in functional neuroanatomy is valuable for personalized diagnostics and therapeutics in psychiatry. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results underscore the importance of considering functional neuroanatomy for personalized diagnostics and therapeutics in psychiatry. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the preprint's interpretive conclusion about the value of considering functional neuroanatomy for personalized care without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sequence analyses provide more accurate predictions of variant effects than predictions of stability. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sequence analyses give more accurate predictions of variant effects than predictions of stability. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same comparative result between sequence-based and stability-based predictions without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: About half of the variants that show loss of function do so due to stability effects. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: About half of the variants that show loss of function do so due to stability effects. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in preprint and publication about the proportion of LOF variants attributable to stability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A machine learning model can predict variant effects from protein structure and sequence alignments. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A machine learning model can predict variant effects from protein structure and sequence alignments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both documents present the same contribution/result that a model can predict variant effects from structure and sequence data.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Large-scale experimental assessments of variant effects can provide deeper insights into the mechanisms that cause loss of function. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Large-scale experimental assessments of variant effects can provide deeper insights into the mechanisms that cause loss of function. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the preprint's conclusion about the value of large-scale experimental assays for mechanistic insight.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The two sources of information from structure and sequence alignments support one another and enable mechanistic interpretations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This explicit statement about the complementary nature of structure and sequence information and mechanistic interpretation appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We present a unified classification of mouse retinal ganglion cells (RGCs) into 42 types based on visually-evoked responses. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We present a unified classification of mouse retinal ganglion cells (RGCs) into 42 types based on visually evoked responses. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same primary result about a 42-type functional classification based on visual responses.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We aligned the functional classification of RGCs to publicly available morphological and transcriptomic data sets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We align the functional classification of RGCs to publicly available morphological and transcriptomic datasets. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The content is the same, but the publication reports a lower stated certainty (high -> medium), so the claim is clarified with tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We created an online database that allows users to browse or download data and classify RGCs from their light responses using a machine learning algorithm. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We create an online database that allows users to browse or download data and classify RGCs from their light responses using a machine learning algorithm. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the same contribution of an online database and classification tool with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work provides a resource for studies of RGCs and establishes a framework for future efforts in neuronal classification and open data distribution. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work provides a resource for studies of RGCs, their upstream circuits in the retina, and their projections in the brain. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the scope (explicitly mentioning upstream circuits and projections) and presents the claim with higher certainty, broadening and strengthening the original resource/framework conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Three Arabidopsis methylases that direct H3K4me1 have been identified. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Three Arabidopsis methyltransferases that direct H3K4me1 have been identified. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication state the same identification of three methyltransferases directing H3K4me1.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: One of the identified enzymes cooperates with the transcription machinery. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: One of the identified methyltransferases cooperates with the transcription machinery. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same finding about one enzyme cooperating with transcription machinery without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The localization patterns of the two other enzymes are associated with specific histone modifications and DNA sequences. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The other two methyltransferases are associated with specific histone modifications and DNA sequences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same associations but reports the result with higher certainty, clarifying and strengthening the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: H3K4me1/2/3 are established and maintained via interplay with transcription and other chromatin features. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: H3K4me1/2/3 are established and maintained via interplay with transcription and other chromatin features. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same mechanistic conclusion about interplay between transcription and chromatin features for H3K4 methylation states.
